channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government urged to implement long-term solutions to reduce flood damage
item.description: Heavy rains across Kenya have caused serious flooding, particularly in Nairobi, coastal regions, and western Kenya, leading to deaths, displacement, and property destruction. The floods have also disr...

item.title: US Court Convicts Pakistani in Iran-Linked Assassination Plot Against US Officials
item.description: A US federal court in Brooklyn has convicted Asif Raza Merchant, a Pakistani national, for his role in a plot allegedly linked to Iran to target former President Donald Trump and other US officials. T...

item.title: Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie barred from Royal Ascot due to family controversy
item.description: Princesses Beatrice, 37, and Eugenie, 35, have reportedly been informed that they will not be allowed to attend Royal Ascot this summer due to their father, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor's, and mother Sa...

item.title: Two Arrested After Suspicious Devices Thrown During Protests Outside NYC Mayor’s Residence
item.description: During protests outside Gracie Mansion, the official residence of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani, tensions between opposing groups escalated, resulting in the deployment of ignited suspicious devi...

item.title: PM Modi Praises Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla as Outstanding and Impartial Ahead of Opposition's No-Confidence Motion
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday heaped praise on Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, describing him as an outstanding Member of Parliament and an excellent Speaker who functions above party lines. Sp...

item.title: Ontario man with paranoid schizophrenia granted temporary cannabis access under supervision
item.description: Michael Stewart, a 46-year-old man from Ontario diagnosed with paranoid schizophrenia, was found not criminally responsible for the 2002 murder of his mother, June Stewart, following a severe psychoti...

item.title: Babaylan: Exploring the Meaning and Cultural Significance of This Filipino Term
item.description: On March 8, 2026, ABS-CBN Lifestyle featured 'babaylan' as its Word of the Day, shining a light on an important piece of Philippine heritage. Babaylan refers to the traditional spiritual leaders, sham...

item.title: Bomet Women Rep Linet Toto Vows to Support Education of Nick Kosgei's Sister
item.description: During the burial of Nick Kosgei in Bomet County, Bomet Women Representative Linet Toto made a heartfelt promise to support the education of one of Nick's younger sisters, who is currently in form fou...

item.title: Toronto experiences widespread power outage in eastern and southwestern neighbourhoods
item.description: On March 7, 2026, a significant power outage affected several areas in Toronto, including the east end, East York, and southwest Scarborough. Residents reported sudden loss of electricity, impacting h...

item.title: Tanzania explores joining SASSCAL to enhance climate research and sustainable land use
item.description: Tanzania dey plan to work closer with the Southern African Science Service Centre for Climate Change and Adaptive Land Management (SASSCAL) to improve scientific research, protect environment and figh...

item.title: Filipino Seafarers May Decline Assignments in Conflict Zones in the Middle East
item.description: The Department of Migrant Workers (DMW) has clarified that Filipino seafarers have the right to refuse deployment to areas considered 'warlike operations zones' in the Middle East, particularly in reg...

item.title: Trump criticises Starmer over delayed UK support for US strikes on Iran
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump has sharply criticised UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer over what he perceives as a delayed response to US-led military actions against Iran. Trump remarked on social me...

item.title: Ruto Orders Relief Support and Hospital Coverage for Nairobi Flood Victims
item.description: President William Ruto has directed swift government action following heavy rains that caused flash floods in Nairobi and parts of Kitui County. The downpour on March 6 led to submerged roads, trapped...

item.title: UPSC 2025 Results Highlight Female Candidates’ Strong Performance
item.description: The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has declared the results for the 2025 examination. Among the top 25 candidates, 11 are women, showcasing a significant representation of female achievers in ...

item.title: Why Gen Z and Millennials Are Turning to Journaling and Stationery
item.description: Analogue hobbies like journaling, scrapbooking, and other stationery pursuits are experiencing a revival among Gen Z and Millennials. Once considered old-fashioned, these activities are now seen as a ...

item.title: Sonja Josiah Ntuli Takes Office as Mpumalanga DPP
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has officially appointed Sonja Josiah Ntuli as the Director of Public Prosecutions (DPP) in Mpumalanga, following the processes stipulated under section 13(1)(c) of the Natio...

item.title: Iran Apologises to Gulf Neighbours Amid Ongoing US-Israel Strikes and Trump Threats
item.description: The Middle East conflict involving Iran, the United States, and Israel has entered its second week, with both sides exchanging intense attacks. Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian issued a rare apolog...

item.title: The link between gut microbes and cardiovascular health
item.description: Recent research highlights the significant role the gut microbiome plays in cardiovascular health. The gut is home to a variety of microbes that influence digestion, immune function, metabolism, and e...

item.title: First Lady Opens Modern Elderly Centre in Akwa Ibom, Urges Law to Support Senior Citizens
item.description: For Ikono Local Government Area, Akwa Ibom State, Nigerian First Lady, Senator Oluremi Tinubu, don officially open one correct Geriatric home wey dem call ‘Oluremi Tinubu Senior Citizens Center’. Duri...

item.title: Indian Navy may be deployed to assist stranded ships in Persian Gulf amid regional tensions
item.description: The Indian government is evaluating the deployment of Indian Navy vessels to escort merchant ships stranded in the Persian Gulf due to regional tensions. A senior government source has stated that a f...
